key_insight: A mass-lumped midpoint finite element method for the Landau--Lifshitz--Gilbert equation with Dzyaloshinskii--Moriya interaction is provably structure-preserving, enforcing unit-length magnetization and achieving optimal first-order spatial and second-order temporal convergence.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a reliable, geometry-preserving numerical tool for simulating chiral micromagnetic dynamics such as skyrmion motion in materials with DMI.
method: Mass-lumped midpoint finite element discretization for exchange + bulk DMI
Exact nodal unit-length preservation with a discrete energy law
Structure-preserving fixed-point solver with residual-based stopping criterion
evidence_signal: The paper provides a priori error analysis and numerical experiments showing that the inexact solver matches the ideal scheme’s convergence rate up to solver tolerance.
watch_item: The method requires sufficient solution regularity and careful residual control in the nonlinear solver to retain the theoretical convergence guarantees.
paper_title: A priori error analysis of a mass-lumped midpoint finite element method with a structure-preserving solver for the Landau--Lifshitz--Gilbert equation with Dzyaloshinskii--Moriya interaction
